rationale: Adding serialVersionUID to exception class; Java-specific serialization concern, no spec behavior involved.
Object equality bug in legacy read preference handling; internal implementation flaw, no specific spec rule contradicted.
User-reported query returns wrong results due to incorrect query construction; support question, not a spec nonconformance.
Concurrency regression causing corrupted wire messages on reconnect; implementation bug, no specific spec rule violated.
Unintentional use of MXBean breaking Java 5 compatibility; internal API regression, no spec involvement.
Disabling checkMaster for slaveOk queries; internal optimization with no clearly contradicted spec rule.
User-reported EOFException during cursor iteration; support question / connection issue, no spec violation.
Race condition regression: secondary reads fail until first write on mongos list; implementation bug, no spec rule named.
DBCursor thread-safety issue; internal concurrency bug, no spec-defined behavior contradicted.
Adding test coverage tooling; build/CI infrastructure, no spec involvement.
Connection timeout option ignored in ConnectionStatus; configuration bug, no cross-driver spec rule contradicted.
$readPreference meta operator missing from command queries to mongos; spec requires drivers to include it for proper routing.
MongoURI ignores username/password/database; missing feature implementation rather than a named spec rule violation.
URI parsing improvement for malformed input; no specific spec rule requiring this error behavior.
MongoOptions.getWriteConcern ignores j field; internal API bug but no specific write concern spec rule clearly contradicted.
Improving exception message when connection pool queue is exhausted; UX improvement, no spec behavior change.
BSON can't serialize BigInteger; Java type mapping gap, BSON spec doesn't define BigInteger handling.
ObjectId serialization incompatibility with Adobe BlazeDS; Java-specific framework interop issue, not a BSON spec violation.
Sort.Direction.DESC enum returns wrong value; internal Java enum bug unrelated to any MongoDB spec.
aggregate/mapReduce commands break when read preference set; spec requires $query wrapping for commands sent with $readPreference.
User reports connection refused; support question about network configuration, no spec involvement.
DBCursor.next() throws RuntimeException instead of NoSuchElementException; Java Iterator contract issue, not a MongoDB spec.
Intermittent read timeout; user configuration/network issue, no spec violation.
Internal feature to disable key validation for legacy data; explicitly for internal use, not a spec conformance issue.
GridFS cursor not closed in finally block; resource management bug, GridFS spec doesn't mandate cursor lifecycle details.
DBRef with Object _id adds spurious $id field; internal serialization bug with no clear BSON spec rule contradicted.
JSON.parse() incorrectly handles backslash in regex strings; JSON parsing bug, not a MongoDB spec concern.
JSON.parse() doesn't handle $binary extended JSON; missing feature, BSON extended JSON handling not fully spec-covered here.
MongoURI should configure SSL socket factory from URI option; improvement, no specific spec rule about SSL factory configuration.
Improve logging at Mongo instance construction; observability improvement, no spec involvement.
JSON.parse() doesn't handle $timestamp extended JSON; missing feature, not a contradiction of a named spec rule.
JSONCallback applies decoding hooks only for ObjectId, not all types; internal encoding hook behavior, no specific spec rule violated.
User confusion about writeResult.getN() return value; support question, no spec violation identified.
Binary class missing equals/hashCode; Java API ergonomics, no BSON spec requirement for these methods.
DBCursor.next() throws 'no more' exception; user misuse of cursor API, support question, no spec violation.
Auth uses primaryPreferred so it doesn't fail when primary unreachable; behavior improvement, no spec rule explicitly violated.
User question about findAndModify behavior; support question, no spec violation.
ReadPreference.nearest() includes primary unexpectedly; server selection spec defines nearest as lowest latency across all members.
NEAREST read preference sends reads to RECOVERING secondary; server selection spec requires excluding RECOVERING nodes from eligible set.
Improving GLE exception to include full document; error reporting improvement, no specific write concern spec rule contradicted.
Duplicate of JAVA-769; user query construction question, support ticket, no spec violation.
Cursor-not-found exception reports wrong cursor id; error reporting bug, no specific spec rule about cursor id in error messages.
Adding convenience methods to MongoClientOptions; API ergonomics improvement, no spec involvement.
Deprecating legacy MongoURI, MongoOptions, Mongo constructors; API housekeeping, no spec involvement.
JMX MBean naming convention mismatch causing registration error; monitoring infrastructure bug, no MongoDB spec involvement.
Secondary-eligible commands whitelist uses camelCase but driver emits lowercase, causing commands to always route to primary against spec intent.
Request to merge URI options with programmatic options; API usability improvement, no spec violation.
Expose MongoClientURI constructor for default options override; API ergonomics, no spec rule contradicted.
Too many open files on Linux; OS resource limit / socket leak, no spec involvement.
aggregate/mapReduce commands ignore read preference set on collection; server selection spec requires honoring collection-level read preference.
